channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Florida man files lawsuit against Netflix over Tyson-Paul fight streaming issues
item.description: A class action lawsuit was filed against Netflix over alleged streaming issues during the fight between legendary brawler Mike Tyson and former YouTuber Jake Paul.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Court ruling stops Louisiana from requiring Ten Commandments in classrooms for now
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Louisiana's plan to make all of the state's public school classrooms post the Ten Commandments remains on hold under an order Wednesday by a federal appeals court in New Orleans. The 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Explicit traffic signs appear in Colorado, police investigating
item.description: The signs used explicit language telling drivers to "slow down [expletive]" and "put down the phone [expletive].
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Two arrested in connection to 15 dead dogs found in south Austin creek
item.description: Investigators executed a search warrant at the suspects' new house in Cameron, and found dead dogs and "bird-like animals" inside.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, NewsNation

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Amid X exodus, Bluesky CEO says 1M people per day joined in past week
item.description: The social media platform Bluesky has emerged as a popular alternative for users departing Elon Musk's X and other platforms, with nearly 1 million people joining each day over the past week, Bluesky CEO Jay Graber said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: You can soon buy a whole jug of this McDonald's product
item.description: The McRib News Gods continue to give. Earlier this week, it was announced that McDonald's McRib would be making its triumphant—yet limited—return to the menu. A press release from McDonald's said some restaurants will see it on Dec 3. Now, the BBQ icing on the cake.
item.dc:creator: Anna Roberts, KTLA 5

item.title: Donald Trump's lawyers urge judge to ignore prosecutors and dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: A day after New York prosecutors said they will fight efforts to dismiss Donald Trump’s hush money conviction, the president-elect’s lawyers urged a judge to ignore them and dispose of the case before he takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FTX co-founder gets no prison time after cooperating in case against Sam Bankman-Fried
item.description: An apologetic FTX co-founder has been sentenced to no time in prison after a prosecutor and a judge praised his cooperation against Sam Bankman-Fried and his efforts to recover money for victims of the cryptocurrency fraud.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuelan migrant gang Tren de Aragua now operating in 16 states: report
item.description: The Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua (TdA) is now operating in 16 states, according to a new Homeland Security memo cited by the New York Post.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Laken Riley murder: Judge sentences college student killer after family addresses 'monster' in court
item.description: A Georgia judge on Wednesday reached a verdict for Jose Ibarra, the suspect accused of murdering Augusta University student Laken Riley on the University of Georgia campus in February.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump has called for dismantling the Education Department. Here's what that would mean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Throughout his campaign, President-elect Donald Trump heaped scorn on the federal Department of Education, describing it as being infiltrated by “ radicals, zealots and Marxists.” He has picked Linda McMahon, a former wrestling executive, to lead the department.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nancy Mace: Capitol transgender bathroom rules protect women
item.description: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace said she's going to "fight like hell" for a resolution to keep Sarah McBride from using the women's bathroom.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Human smuggling trial witness says he shuttled over 500 Indian migrants into the US
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — A man convicted of human smuggling testified Tuesday that he shuttled more than 500 Indian migrants across the U.S.-Canada border over four years as part of an international smuggling ring that prosecutors said led to the deaths of a family of four.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mattel releases doll honoring EGOT winner Rita Moreno
item.description: Toy maker Mattel is releasing a doll honoring Emmy, Grammy, Oscar and Tony (EGOT) winner Rita Moreno, a prominent Latina actor.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Texas offers Trump 1,400 borderland acres for deportation plans
item.description: Texas Land Commissioner Dawn Buckingham offered 1,402 acres in Starr County to the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Survivors of LGBTQ+ club shooting say the deaths and trauma could've been avoided in lawsuits
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Adriana Vance collected herself in front of the cameras, two years to the day after her son was killed in the mass shooting at an LGBTQ+ nightclub in Colorado Springs, a murder that lawsuits allege could have been prevented by law enforcement and the club's owners.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These are the best cruise lines for 2025
item.description: If you're looking to spend a vacation at sea but haven't decided which cruise line is best for your specific needs, a new online ranking shows the best options for every type of traveler, from families to luxury seekers. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Today's entry-level employees not ready for the job, survey of executives says
item.description: (KRON) -- Entry-level Gen Z workers aren't prepared for the demands of the modern workplace, according to a majority of executives and workers surveyed in a new report.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: 51-year-old man is charged with murder after 3 are stabbed in New York City rampage
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man has been charged with three counts of first-degree murder in the fatal stabbings of three people in a string of random attacks across Manhattan, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nvidia rivals focus on building a different kind of chip to power AI products
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates the market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former 'American Idol' contestant pleads guilty in deadly DUI crash
item.description: Former "American Idol" contestant Caleb Kennedy has pleaded guilty to driving under the influence during a deadly 2022 crash in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Robert Cox, Fox 5

item.title: Trump assassination attempt task force subpoenas 2 ATF employees, alleging stonewalling
item.description: Congressional lawmakers have accused the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) of stonewalling their requests for documents on the assassination attempts against Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mountain lion rescued from tree outside California home
item.description: First responders rescued a young mountain lion from a tree after he wandered into the yard of an Orange County home on Monday afternoon
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Laken Riley murder: Family of slain Georgia student sobs in court as witnesses describe crime scene evidence
item.description: Georgia prosecutors placed Jose Ibarra, the suspect accused of murdering Augusta University nursing student Laken Riley, at the scene of the crime on the morning of Feb. 22.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania's high court orders counties not to count disputed ballots in US Senate race
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania's state Supreme Court on Monday weighed in on a flashpoint amid ongoing vote counting in the U.S. Senate election between Democratic Sen.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 people are killed in random stabbings in New York City. A suspect is in custody
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man fatally stabbed three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, carrying out a series of random attacks without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Speaker Johnson's 'epic' weekend with Trump shows strengths and limits of his power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was House Speaker Mike Johnson walking behind President-elect Donald Trump's entourage into Saturday night's UFC fight at Madison Square Garden, his stature overcome by the enormity of the scene around him. And Johnson mugging with musicians Kid Rock and Jelly Roll.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal appeals court won't block upcoming nitrogen gas execution in Alabama
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — A federal appeals court said Monday it will let the nation’s third execution with nitrogen gas go forward this week in Alabama, rejecting arguments that the new method causes unconstitutional levels of pain. The 11th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man attacked by owls twice in less than a week
item.description: Owls aren't notoriously menacing creatures, but those living in the Old Shell Road area might not agree.
item.dc:creator: Asher Redd, WFLA, NBC

item.title: About 20% of Americans regularly get their news from influencers on social media, report says
item.description: About one in five Americans – and a virtually identical share of Republicans and Democrats – regularly get their news from digital influencers who are more likely to be found on the social media platform X, according to a report released Monday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to stop using the same password over and over
item.description: The most common password is 123456. How long does it take to crack that? Less than one second. That's the findings from NordPass, which has been studying the most common passwords for six years. It's time to start using a password manager. Here's how.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5
